summary: applyCoinResponseTransforms now deletes the stub Images: [{}, {}] key from every CoinFactsModel response. Wired into coin facts-cert (already calling the transform), coin facts-grade (already calling), coin facts-barcode (gap from PR #683 — transform call added here), and coin batch (live + cached). HasObverseImage / HasReverseImage / HasTrueViewImage / ImageReady booleans are preserved; image URLs continue to be fetched via `coin images <cert>`.
reason: PCGS returns a 2-element stub array with no URL fields on every CoinFactsModel response when an image exists. The empty objects read as 'the image fetch failed' to a first-time consumer; the AGENTS.md P6 locked-in fix is option 2 (omit entirely) because option 1 doubles quota per call (violates VISION.md's 1,000/day budget) and option 3 (null) leaves the same confusion in a different shape. Safe: the envelope classifier (classifyPCGSEnvelope) already consumes the Images=[] + Has*Image=false heuristic earlier in resolveRead, so stripping at the response-transform layer cannot affect bogus-cert detection on the GetImagesByCertNo endpoint.
validated_outcome: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...; live smoke: `coin facts-cert 50483263 --agent | jq '.data | has("Images")'` returns false; `jq '.data.HasTrueViewImage'` returns true; `coin images 50483263 --agent | jq type` returns "object"
